config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by the state or the majority. In my view, the real test of a just society is whether it protects people even when they are unpopular, inconvenient, or in the minority. From my perspective, collective interests are important, but they should be balanced with strong individual rights, not placed above them by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because once that rule is taken too far, it becomes very easy to justify abuse in the name of order or efficiency. From my perspective as someone from China, I do understand why people emphasize collective stability, public safety, and social harmony, since those values are deeply familiar and often genuinely important in daily life. But I do not think that makes individual rights secondary by default, because a society that ignores personal dignity and basic protections can become rigid and unfair, even if it looks orderly on the surface. The better approach is to balance them case by case, with clear limits on what society can demand from a person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual by default, because in my experience that is exactly how people excuse coercion and unfair treatment. In Indonesia, we are taught to value gotong royong and social harmony, and I respect that, but I also know how easily "for the common good" can be used to pressure minorities or silence criticism. A society is only truly healthy when it can protect public interests without treating individual rights as disposable. So yes, balance matters, but the individual must have strong protection even when the majority wants something else.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not take priority over the rights of the individual by default. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, harmony, and collective goals, because those ideas are very familiar and often genuinely useful in real life, but I have also seen how easily “social interest” can become a blanket excuse for pressure and unfairness. The point about minorities and dissent is especially important: if individual rights can be overridden whenever the majority or the state says it is for the common good, then those rights are not really secure. A better principle is that society can ask for cooperation, but it must still respect clear limits that protect personal dignity and basic freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual by default, because that is too easy to abuse in the name of harmony, order, or efficiency. From my Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of gotong royong and social responsibility, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or pressure people who already have less power. Society is important, but it is not automatically moral just because it is collective; a just society must protect individual dignity, especially for minorities and dissenters. So I would keep the balance, but I would put strong limits on how far society can go when it conflicts with basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not outrank the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, public order, and collective goals, because those concerns are real and often necessary, but I have also seen how easily “the common good” can be used to justify pressure on ordinary people. If individual rights can be set aside whenever the majority or authorities claim it is useful, then those rights are too weak to protect minorities or dissenters. A good society should ask people to contribute, but it must still draw firm lines around personal dignity and basic freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
